trivia_bullets.text: Tarpan is a {G} 1/1 Horse creature from Ice Age (ICE, June 1995), set on the frozen Dominarian continent of Terisiare. The Ice Age setting represents the aftermath of the Brothers' War: glaciers, blizzards, and survival cultures including the militaristic Kjeldorans and the nomadic Balduvians. Tarpan gained a reprint in Fifth Edition (1997). [Scryfall]

Flavor text: "A good Tarpan will serve you, faithful and true. A bad one will kick you in the head." — General Jarkeld, the Arctic Fox. Jarkeld was a Kjeldoran military commander famed for tactical brilliance; the 'Arctic Fox' epithet refers to his cunning. According to MTG Wiki search results, he was presumed slain in battle in the Adarkar Wastes. The quote is pragmatic soldier's wisdom: a useful horse is an asset, a bad one is a liability — straightforward Ice Age survivalism in 21 words. [Scryfall; MTG Wiki: Jarkeld via WebSearch]

The tarpan is a real-world extinct wild horse subspecies (Equus ferus ferus) that roamed the Eurasian steppe. The last scientifically confirmed wild individual was killed in 1879; the last captive individual died in Russia in 1918. The name derives from the Kazakh word for 'wild horse.' Ice Age drew on real-world Ice Age fauna and geography for its creature roster — naming a 1/1 Horse after an extinct wild horse is consistent with the set's naturalistic flavor design. [Wikipedia: Tarpan, via WebSearch]

Tarpan's death trigger — gain 1 life when it dies — is a minimal consolation ability on a 1/1 for one mana. Its EDHREC rank of 27,151 reflects very low Commander inclusion, as expected for a vanilla-adjacent creature with no attack or synergy upside. It is legal in Legacy, Vintage, Commander, and Pauper. [Scryfall]
